text: This past week I made a guana tacos and it really shook the internet and I got a whole bunch of hate so let's talk about ethical eating. Now I totally get if you just won't eat a guana taco everybody has their limits that's fine. But overall that wasn't the main issue that people had in the comments so let's talk about it. Now when I made the original video I said that a guana's are an invasive species here in South Florida but just in case anyone's not sure of the criteria this is it specifically here in South Florida this is the impact that a guana's have on our local ecosystem. Due to the extreme cold temperatures we had this week that immobilized a lot of them in Florida fish and wildlife had drop off points and collected over 5,000 of them most of which ended up being euthanized. So rather than let the one that I found go to waste I decided to make tacos and I would argue that it's the most ethical taco you could possibly have. Locally hunting and sourcing your own food in general saves the entire planet from greenhouse gases of your food having to travel around the world to get to your plate. When it comes to invasive species then you're not only helping the entire planet by not flying it across the world but you're also helping your local ecosystem. That brings us to point number two. You all do realize that chicken and beef and all of that comes from somewhere right? It comes from farms and personally after seeing the conditions that many of these animals are in at these farms I think that these have it way better off. They have a great life and then one bad day rather than some of the animals on these farms having an awful life. And that brings us to point number three. Why don't you just go vegan? And personally I don't have any beef with vegans but I also will say that I do think that this is also better than even going vegan and here's why. A lot of our produce, fruits and vegetables as well as vegan products come from monocultured crops and those have to grow somewhere which means that areas for wildlife habitat have to be bulldozed in order to do so. And then on top of that they have to fly over to get to your table. Hopefully this explains the why behind my iguana tacos and challenges people to think about where their food comes from. Until next time get outside and see what you can find.
